text: Jessa Drops a Bomb
29 ABY
Jessalyn Valios
Bespin
Atsvara Tarasar (NPC), Crestian Tarasar, Elana Tracer, Jessalyn Valios, Johanna Siri te Danaan, Kyyel Marrak
Jedi, Caspian Union, Galactic Imperium
A chance meeting at Johanna's apartment on Cloud City...

%tThe afternoon skies over Bespin weren't a sight Kyyel had seen much of before; if anything, the planet had been a point to jump from port before heading to another location, and even then, he couldn't ever remember staying longer than it took to fill the tanks. Now, he had the sensation of no longer having the freedom to leave as he wished... at least, not under his own power. The Corellian in him was somewhat annoyed at the concept... taking public shuttles sounded just a hairs worse than bumming a ride from an old friend. He simply took a moment to shove the thought aside. He was, at least, back. And for the most part it seemed nearly everyone was doing well. Still, the weight of time that he had been gone hung over him. For the moment, he was simply out on one of the numerous balconies of Johanna's suite, kneeling in silent contemplation and trying to not be too great a burden or impose too heavily. However, given her amassed wealth, that seemed a futile and foolish thought. He looked over his thread-bared robes for a moment, and allowed a faint smile before his eyes fell on the metal cylinder that was laid out before him. He had tried to focus for a few moments, his brow knitting in concentration as he took the mental exercises again... and again...%r%tNothing. Resigned, he shook his head, and then lifted the lightsaber by hand to clasp it on the swordbelt under his robes. It was good to be back among them. He focused out as the skies began to darken, as day turned to night.

%tIt's early evening, and after returning to the city, Jessalyn rendezvoused with her children and Lofwarr, their faithful guardian and friend, for dinner in the Hotel. They headed out for more meetups and parties with their friends afterwards, and Jessalyn finds herself heading to the penthouse to find Johanna. She approaches the door and hesitates, rubbing her forehead and trying not to notice how her hand shakes when she reaches for the buzzer.

%tThings on Bespin had devolved the previous evening for Elana Tracer. After Tyler left, Johanna had disappeared into her bedroom presumably - there were so many doors it was hard to say. Then she'd stayed and drank with Crestian and Kyyel for a while and gone back to her suite to fall heavily into her bed. But she woke to the same internal strife she thought she'd drank away the night before. Had the sudden mutual attraction between Tracer's son Kipran and Johanna's ward - and Tyler's daughter - Sonja and Johanna's lack of information on it put a rift in their friendship. Elana couldn't leave Cloud City without speaking to her face to face once more.%r%tWhile her IS2D was being readied for her departure and Lon and Ashton were doing last minute shopping before returning to Caspar, Elana spends her morning eating breakfast in her suite and slowly packing for everyone - except for Kipran. He had left with Tyler earlier to return to Selene. Once she's all squared away and can make no more excuses to avoid seeing her fellow Moff and friend, Tracer makes her way back to the penthouse floor while her unneeded security detail take her belongings to the /Sapphire Star/.%r%tUpon her arrival, Tracer sees Jessalyn and stops, scratching the back of her head uncomfortably. "Oh...I was just coming to see Joh." She chuckles with a bit of nerves. "Of course, you probably are too. I...I could come back later...." Even though she had less than two hours left before her scheduled departure.

%tThe wonderful thing about Crestian's position in the galaxy was it really did not matter where in the galaxy he was. There was always some work to be done to benefit Caspian foreign policy to work to strengthen the wider Imperium. Still there were matters back in Caspian space calling his attention and lingering half a galaxy away was starting to prove some distraction. The Leviathan had been all squared away to take the Caspian contigent back to their homeworld but like the others he felt some calling to pay his respects to their host before his departure. While others had their children off elsewhere Crestian decided to bring his daughter Atsvara along. As they seemingly reach the room at the same time as everyone else he remarks to Jessalyn and Tracer, "It was my idea to come here right? We weren't just all secretly summoned by the Force?"

%t"I did say you were free to stay as long as you liked," a familiar feminine voice suddenly notes from several paces behind the meditating Corellian, "And I mean what I say, otherwise it's not worth the yapping, is it?" It's Johanna, of course, wearing one of the dark and elaborate outfits she favors on Bespin, hands clasped sedately in front of her abdomen while her gaze tunnels through Kyyel's back. "You'll need a ship, of course... if you have any particular type in mind, I'll arrange for one to be delivered to Cloud City." Whether she's just listened to the surface chatter in his thoughts or whether she's merely done her research is hard to say; the Jedi offers no explanation either way ere she retreats from the balcony to answer the door, Jessalyn's Force-presence like a beacon flanked by two more muted, but equally welcome energies in the form of Elana and Crestian. "Well this is a pleasant surprise," she says brightly, admitting the guests into the suite.

%tKyyel's smile grew as he heard Johanna behind him, and he turned his head slightly to look over one shoulder at her, "Heh... they still making the yt-2400? Or did I miss that getting decommissioned?" As she leaves to answer the door, he stays for a moment until he hears her welcome. "Well, best not be too strange a guest." He rose smoothly to his feet and then made his way over towards the main room. As he saw who was standing in the doorway, his eyes widened in surprise. "Huh, did I miss a dinner invitation?" the tall Corellian stood in clear view while waiting in the main room, his grin a greeting as much as his words. "Jessalyn, Elana... Crestian. Good to see you all again."

%tThe arrival of the two others definitely was not what Jessalyn had in mind for this meeting, and she's tempted to make an escape before the door can open. "Elana," she says to the other woman, managing a smile for her. At least they had had a chance to break the ice for a moment the previous day, but she has no such warm gaze for the Presav. "I didn't now she was expecting others. I'll see myself back -- " But the door zips open and she folds her arms over her chest as Johanna bids them to come inside. "I'm sorry, Joh," she says as she steps into the chamber. Her face is strained and pale in contrast to her bright hair that's pulled back into a very messy bun. "I didn't realize you were having a meeting. I can come back later."

%tElana smiles as Joh greets them all so warmly, then she chuckles at Crestian and moves past Jessalyn and into the suite. With a shrug and a look back at Crestian, she moves further inside. While Joh speaks with the Jedi Master, Tracer comes upon Kyyel first, giving him a friendly hug, patting his back. "So good to see you again, Jinx," she says, slowly turning towards the windows, awestruck by the beauty of the clouds and colorful sky beyond. "I do wish we could stay longer but...I've got so much to do." She turns back to Kyyel. "So now that you're um...back...have you thought about where you might settle?" Small talk for now - that was nice wasn't it?

%tWhatever Crestian's thoughts were on Jessalyn's presence he endeavoured to keep them muted both in his mind and on his expression. The whole nature of the dance had meant interaction with her had been expected and though he had no expectation the Jedi Order would wish to accept any asylum offer from Caspar, Crestian was well aware in making it that it would include her. He had kept any talk of their animosity out of his discussions with his government. Ushering himself and his daughter into the suite the Caspian leader could not help but imagine that Atsvara might finally get to do some chaperoning after all, just not all the way she intended. The mention of a meeting, however, garners a reply, "Meeting? Well I do believe Atsvara and I were just coming to pay our respects before returning to Caspar." He looks to Tracer and adds in question, "Still Caspar bound yourself or headed to back the Core." He nods politely to Kyyel and Johanna as well while his daughter capes at the view again.

%t"I wasn't, actually," Johanna chuckles when Jessalyn mentions a meeting, a comforting hand placed between the redhead's shoulder blades while the pilot guides her towards a plush chair and then gestures for Elana, Crestian, Atsvara and Kyyel to make themselves comfortable as well once they've greeted one another. "Looks like the Force brought us all together, so we might as well take advantage of a little company. In truth I wasn't expecting to see anyone other than ol' Marrak over there," she concludes with a wink before moving to kneel before Crestian's daughter so she might address the girl on her own level, "And what a lovely surprise it is to have this young lady with us, too. Did you have fun at the dance, Atsvara?"

%tElana's hug was returned with a tight one of his own, "Good to see you too, Zephyr. Especially before you have to take off." Kyyel looked to the young girl and gave her a quick nod, "Hey there. Pleased to meet you." He looked up towards Crestian, and then smirked faintly. "Not sure just yet. Though at least it seems the ship issue will be taken care of, so I'll at least be able to pick where to go next. As for where to settle? Who knows... in all honesty." He glanced to Elana before looking out to the group, "I'm still finding my feet where everything is. The temple still where I left it?" Nevermind the fact that he wasn't even sure if it would be appropriate for him. Or if he'd even be welcomed. The worry that crossed his mind nearly reached his eyes, but he turned to give Johanna a thankful look, "Much obliged, by the way, for the offer of a ship. After losing the Aegis I haven't been behind much of anything you'd want to say is actually flight worthy."

%tRelenting for the moment, Jessalyn smiles at her friend's warm reception and glances over to see Kyyel and Tracer speaking together once again as Johanna greets the little girl that Tarasar brought with him, the two doing an excellent job of ignoring one another. She pauses to smile at Johanna's interaction with the child, however, then crosses to Moff Tracer and Kyyel, offering a nod to each of them in turn. "I'm glad you're still here. We should talk, my old friend," she says to her former apprentice. His mention of the Temple brings a wan smile to her face. "Things have... things -are- changing," she says to him quietly. "But wherever we end up, you'll always be welcome. Don't ever doubt that, all right?"

%tTracer steps away from Kyyel and finds a spot on a sofa as she answers Crestian. "That's why I came, too. And Caspar, unless Lon and Ash ride back with you..." She considers a direct return to Selene where it would be easier to oversee her projects both old and new. She smiles watching Jessalyn interact with Atsvara, having heard of the girl's interaction with Loy Marin's droid Bee. "They grow up so fast..."

%t"I enjoyed the dance very much, thank you for inviting me," responded the young sar-spotted golden-skinned girl. Manners after all were taught at Miss Cason's School for Sith Spawn. A lack of manners should only ever be employed as tactical decision and not based on a failure to be housebroken by the Dark Lord. "I wish my friend Vee-Won had come though. I think he would have liked to dance with Bee. Zub should make a nice dress for her." The reporter's droid acted matronly, so whether it identified as female or not Atsvara had conceived of it as such. Crestian adds, "I think Astvara was concerned Bee might not be having enough fun."%r%tTurning to back to Tracer he adds, "Well if they need to be back on Selene they are certainly welcome on Leviathan. We would welcome the company." Atsvara grows excited at the notion of the presence of the Tracer spawn. Not that Leviathan was lacking for other children, but newer friends were cooler friends. Crestian turns back to the others adds, "I have been remiss in introductions haven't I." He points to Jinx and says, "This is our new friend Kyyel," the girl bowing slightly in greeting to him. Then as he starts to to point to Jessalyn and opens his mouth Atsvara answers herself, "Sabine's Mum." As much as Crestian might want to ignore Jessalyn he did not exactly want to explain to his daughter why he was.

%tJohanna laughs again, delighted by Atsvara's sweet manner. "Wonderful! You know what? Next time, we'll make sure Vee-Won comes along. You can tell him he's invited to any event that I invite you to, and maybe Miss Bee will pop up again. Then both of you can make her feel as welcome and included as the organics are." Of course, any gathering like this would necessitate locking Cricket in closet with the dreaded restraining bolt. She certainly can't risk him rampaging around, demanding to be let loose among the polite droids of civilized society!

%tKyyel's gratitude earns him a grin from Bespin's Moff, a familiar spark of mischief flashing in her eyes before she rises to her feet and glides over to join Tracer on the sofa. "I wanted to apologize for yesterday," she murmurs, "I didn't mean to imply that I disapprove of Kipran courting Sonja, he's a fine young man and I'm sure the two of them will figure it out. It was just..." Johanna glances down to the hem of her elaborately-beaded overcoat for a moment, as if the right words can be found there, "... it was just that my brother should have told me. I raised her and she's like another daughter, the other children consider her their sister. Tyler and I will have words later... but I'm not angry with you, or with Kipran. Does that make sense?"

%t"Thanks, Jessalyn. That means a lot. And... yeah. We really should have that talk before either of us leave. Though, I at least need to wait for the ship to arrive." There was a sudden weight to his words and tone, but he kept his smile up, especially with Atsvara present. He didn't need to be mopey around the kid. "No need to bow, Atsvara. It's an honor to meet you. I hope you enjoyed the ball with the others." He settled down onto one of the seats, letting himself relax for a bit while he glanced up towards Joh. He listened to the exchange, and frowned slightly in consideration of her words. Finally, he smiled, "Joh, just don't shove his head into a fresher..."

%tJessalyn gives Kyyel a reassuring pat on the arm, sensing his unease and wishing she could tell him more of what she knew -- and what she had Seen. But her thoughts are interrupted by Tarasar making introductions, and she schools her features into a civil enough expression that softens immediately when the child refers to her in reference to her daughter. She swallows hard and crouches down much the same way Johanna did before, smiling to Atsvara on her own level. "That's right. It's nice to meet you, dear one. You can call me Jessalyn, though. I'm not very into fancy titles." Straightening back up, her green gaze sweeps over the room, alighting on each person in turn, and she catches the conversation between Johanna and Tracer regarding their children. She approaches the couch where Tracer and Johanna are seated and holds up an apologetic finger. "You'd be surprised at how understanding Joh can be about such matters, Elana. I wouldn't worry too much about that," she offers, and then pivots to grab both of Johanna's hands, tugging her up off the couch. "And please forgive my rudeness, everyone, but Joh, may I borrow your ear, privately, for just a moment? Can we talk, on the veranda, maybe? And then I will be out of your way."

%tTracer turns to face Johanna when she sits on the sofa with her. Her clear blue eyes cloud a little bit and she reaches out to squeeze Johanna's hand. "No..no...I completely understand. I'm sorry that I didn't know...that no one had told you. I figured Sonja at least..." Maybe Johanna hadn't seen her daughter. Kipran had sent a holo from the Leviathan telling her all about his reassignment and how amazing Sonja was and about how her father was permitting him to court her. Court her. Such an archaic term. "I know you feel as though she's your daughter and he's just ripping her away. How awful." She frowns sympathetically and looks out at the gorgeous view again. "You know...Kipran's father Ben brought me here for our first date...I was nineteen and he swept me off my feet." But then Kyyel chimes in and Elana can't help but laugh. "Oh my no! Please don't," she mockingly begs Johanna before looking a bit more serious. "We'll just promise to let each other know what's going on where the two of them are concerned, alright?" Went Jessalyn intervenes, Elana nods and lets Joh go for now, their peace made and assured.

%t"I do think your brother means well on this in his way. So please just leave him bruised." Crestian smirks and adds, "What was that about being housebroken...." He leaves the point there, something else he does not want to have to explain to his daughter. Making fun of the Imperator was a more at the 9 or 10 year old level of thinking he thought. He consider some platitude about family bonds, needing to all stick together, moving past stuff. But extending it out too far just made his brain hurt. A simple nod is made towards Jessalyn and Johanna as the former wants a private word with the latter and he ends up turning his attention to Kyyel. Perhaps he should offer him a ride, but beyond what kept him thus far on Bespin, he question whether he should push a Caspar visit too much, the man had a lot of catching up to do. Atsvara for her part considered making some comment about fancy titles being fun, but there was enough tension in the room to think better of trying to add Jessalyn to her targets for liberation tonight. She would just have to content herself to working on fancy titles for herself, and one day following in Aunt Elana's footsteps as Moff Balance.

%t"Though it's tempting, I wasn't planning on shoving his face into the 'fresher," the pilot quips, returning Elana's squeeze of the hand and grateful that the other brunette knows how Johanna must feel, "No, we'll stick to words... for /now/. And speaking of words, yes... let's always keep each other updated." Then, to Kyyel: "If you can bear to stay here another few days, your ship issue will be resolved and the Aegis will become nothing more than a distant memory. Three or four days at most and you'll be free to tramp from one end of our wretched galaxy to the other," she notes, "And in case you think I've forgotten that you once made your living behind the stick of something with more bite than a freighter, I haven't. I think a fighter would suit you just fine as a spare, no?"%r%tBut then Jessalyn pulls her to her feet and the talk of children and ships ceases for the moment, Johanna giving the others an apologetic look before she accompanies her old friend out onto the veranda, curiosity and puzzlement ghosting their way through the Force. "Well you certainly have my attention," she says, the pair of them nearly flush with the balustrade, "What's going on?"

%tKyyel laughed and waved a hand at Johanna, "Hey, you put a nice enough engine on a '2400 and she can outmanuver most fighters. Besides, fighters are great, but a place to sleep in long trips is helpful... wait..." he narrowed his eyes, "A -spare-? Joh, how many ships do I need?" He let the matter pause when Jessalyn took her aside and then glanced back towards Elana, "Well while they do that... what are you flying these days, anyway? And you..." He glanced towards Atsvara, "how about you, Atsvara? What's your interest?"

%tOutside on the veranda, the sunset has faded into dusk and the city lights are shining brightly below them as Jessalyn leans out over the balcony, the wind catching her hair and her cloak in a dramatic display. "Joh," is the only thing she says aloud as her friend joins her and she faces her with a somber expression. She rests her hand on top of the other Jedi's on the railing and closes her eyes, finding it all too easy to open her mind to her, the events of the past few days pouring between them in an instant, images and emotions that would have been too difficult to express in words in any case. When she opens her eyes she gives Johanna a long look, then clears her throat and clenches her fingers around hers. "I trust you," she whispers.

%tTracer watches Johanna go off with Jessalyn and sighs even though things worked out. She presses a knuckle into a spot between her brows for a moment, then looks up to find herself in a similar, if dryer, group of friends once again. "I have an MU shuttle...all custom for living inside where the cargo bay should be. It's a bit tight now that the kids are grown. And say...you're gonna make me jealous," the CEO of KDY-SFS teases Kyyel flicking his arm with her hand in admonishment. "I don't have my own fighter!" She looks between Kyyel and Crestian. "Well I don't!" She chucks her head to one side. "I...do test flights whenever I can, though so...that's fun." Elana then turns to hear Atsvara's answer.

%tKyyel's question caught the young girl off guard. As far as she could recall no one had ever asked her what kind of ship she wanted. After a moment of thought she said, "A Millennial Falcon?" She paused then added, "A swoop too. Elana has a big track on Caspar!" Yup she has decided who she wants to be when she grows up or like now. Crestian for his part remarks to Tracer, "You really ought to have your own fighter. You are kind of a big deal you know."

%tSilhouetted against the dusk, the Force arcing between them, the two Jedi enter an intense state of concentration. Jessalyn's physical touch may be gentle but the influx of images is anything but, the impossible speed at which the information hurtles its way from sender to recipient causing Johanna's form to stiffen while her pupils widen in sympathetic response to the jolt of adrenaline wending its way through her body, the sensation of such a transfer not unlike falling from a great height. When the thing is finished mere moments after it began, the redhead's grip on her hand now stronger than before, Johanna finds herself trembling. "I see," she nods, sucking in a steadying breath and exhaling through pursed lips, "I... we'll talk tomorrow, okay?" She looks to where the others are comfortably bantering with one another, their warm and easy conversation a welcome contrast to the cacophony still reverberating in her mind.

%tKyyel nearly laughed at Tracer's comments, "You don't have your own fighter? Seriously? You test flight. Well that's nice... at least you can rent what's on the cutting edge." He winked, good naturedly, and then looked back towards Atsvara, "oooh, good choice. Corellian's make the best ships. That's a pretty nice one too..." he leaned in and grinned, "the trick is... find what's best for you. And I'm willing to bet that your Aunt will take you on a swoop ride soon, if you're good." His eyes caught the return of the two women and he grinned at them, "Seems like we have a future swoop racer here."

%t"Yes, we will talk," Jessalyn agrees on a whisper. Her own head is still swimming, and through the last tendrils of their contact, she conveys her apologies and a gentle touch of the Force meant to soothe at least some of the turmoil she just imparted on her friend. "I'm sorry." She embraces Johanna's shivering form, kissing the side of her hair, and then turns and makes her way from the terrace back into the main chamber. "It was good to see you all again," she says to the group as she prepares to depart. "I hope everyone has a safe journey home, or wherever your next stop may be." She pulls her cloak close around her and pauses at the doorway, slowly lifting her head to glance over at Tarasar. "I hope you're able to find Starker and bring him to justice, Presav," she says in a clear voice. "I may not be able to help you as I would like, but... I know another Jedi who can." With a meaningful glance back to Johanna, she dips out into the corridor and lets the door shut behind her.

%t"Ahhh...but nothing will ever replace my old A-Wing in my heart," the one-time fighter pilot says wistfully. "Slick as can be and fast...so fast," she laughs. "Man I was stupid when I was young. Didn't know how easily...until you experience your first loss..." With a sigh she stands up and heads to the coffee service laid out on a hover tray. "A bit of caff is in order. I still have to fly tonight," she notes while mixing a mostly sweet concoction. Leaning against a counter she grins at Atsvara. "Next time I'm on Caspar if you want," she tells the girl before the two Jedi women return. She watches and gives a nod as Jessalyn departs and her words stun Tracer who then stares at Crestian. "What did she say?"

%tThere had been a lot of noise on Bespin lately but perhaps none as great as the sound of Crestian's jaw dropping to the floor. He looks at the door that Jessalyn just left utterly dumbfounded. He steps behind his daughter, placing his hands over her ears he looks to Johanna. In spite of the hands over his daughter's ears, he only mouth his words, "What the ever loving fuck." He takes his hands off her ears and places one of his hand forehead.%r%t"She literally, literally stood in the way of me doing that." He takes a deep breath, "Does she even know what she is hoping for? My government outlawed him. She hopes I bring him to justice? So she it rooting for me to kill him? I mean that is what it boils down to. Is that the justice she is looking for?" The Caspian looks for one of the plush seats, taking a seat.%r%t"And why exactly isn't this necessary? Wasn't the whole point of allowing the matter to be adjudicated by Tyler excising the Sith Spawn from his head that it would take care of the issue?" This moment sees Crestian wasting no effort to give his boss his proper title. "Has she finally realised that his actual personality is just as dangerous....perhaps more so? Did you all make a mistake in letting him go? I mean my Gods what was the point? Weren't use supposed to have already brought justice?"%r%tAtsvara for her part lingers around her father eventually asking, "Are you talking about the bad man who killed all the people?" Crestian nods solemnly to his daughter.

%t"I don't know what she's hoping for," Johanna shrugs, in no mood to referee a blowup and wishing that Jessalyn could just have left well enough alone, "But whatever you do, it won't happen on Bespin. I said publicly that purging the spawn from Starker would make him human again and my brother accepted my decision that it was sufficient justice /for now/. If you choose to hunt him you will do so outside of my territories, I won't have my decision gainsaid in my own jurisdiction by a fellow Moff. Are we clear?" It's a side of her that Crestian has perhaps never seen before: the de facto ruler squelching anything that might give the impression she has anything less than an iron grip on her holdings. "Don't misunderstand; I shed no tear for Starker should he find himself shot and tossed out of an airlock. Do what you must, Crestian."

%t"Great he is Human again. What the hell good is that? Do you really think that Human is safe for the galaxy and innocent of the reign of terror?" Crestian is rather apoplectic, "But did I mention a thing about him? Have I really do anything to go after him? I haven't placed a bounty. I haven't hired anyone to track him down. I thought we had bigger matters. I have tried to let it rest as best I can. Though I am increasingly unimpressed with this jurisdictional grandstanding. He killed 2.8 billion innocent people and your jurisdiction is happy enough to let that pass because Vadim made him do that and Vadim's hold on him is allegedly broken."%r%tCrestian covers his daughters hears again and mouths, "What the fuck what he doing interacting with Vadim to begin with." Uncovering her ears he continues, "And your dear friend, sister, master was the most willing to give him some second chance, to protect him. And then out of nowhere she leaves saying I hope I can bring him to justice? So yah I was expecting a little insight from you as to why there is no consistency. You letting him go made little sense to me beyond doing it for her. So while in hell would she offer an encouraging word to a Sith with an ax to grind? You do get how insane this all sounds right?"%r%tAs an after thought in slightly calmer tone Crestian turns to Kyyel and responds, "Basca Starker is the man who developed the plague that killed 90 percent of the population of Caspar at the direction of Emperor Vadim....supposedly without his freewill in the matter."

%tKyyel took in the information... amidst the rage that he didn't need the force to feel, at all. Or even Joh's own growing emotions. He frowned and then leaned back, his arms crossing over his chest. "That's some pretty hefty sins to weigh." He chewed on his lower lip in thought, and then exhaled. "...but then again, having felt the loss of free will on occasion... I don't know how I'd react. I don't even know how this man's reacting to what he's done. Jessalyn's thoughts are... often confusing, at first. But I've never seen that they are without reason." He shut his eyes, "A damn plague though..."

%tCrestian takes a deep breath, and another, and another. "If his mind is completely broken then again I just don't see what justice is Jessalyn is looking for from me. He has been outlawed by the Caspian Union. There would be no trial, right now a rabid Kath Hound has more legal standing then he does....well I am guess, I should probably look into what animal cruelty laws we have on the books. And if Jessalyn was implying Johanna could help it would seems she have some idea what Jessalyn brought the matter up for to begin with. I really had just been trying to accept this, begrundingly, as having been adjudicated fully."
Logs
